asset.title: Noderoom Live Collab V1
asset.purpose: Narrated explainer episode. Judge story clarity, visual design, proof quality, and whether it fits the README claim.

judge.summary: An exceptionally clear and well-produced narrated explainer demonstrating multiplayer AI collaboration on a shared spreadsheet substrate, highlighting conflict resolution and state synchronization.

featureClarity.evidence: The video clearly demonstrates how human and AI agents interact on a shared spreadsheet, showing real-time updates and conflict resolution.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the complete sequence from initial prompt, agent execution, human intervention, conflict, and final resolution.

visualDesign.evidence: Clean dark-themed UI with clear visual separation between the chat, spreadsheet, and trace logs.

consistency.evidence: Perfectly aligns with the NodeRoom narrative of collaborative agents, shared artifacts, and execution traces.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Features actual UI interactions, real-time state changes, and code-level explanations of the locking mechanism.

legibility.evidence: Most text is readable, though some spreadsheet cells and code comments are slightly small at standard browser scale.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for developers and product managers designing multi-agent collaborative systems.

productionHonesty.evidence: Honest about the complexities of concurrent edits and explicitly shows the underlying code handling the state.

observedEvidence: Spreadsheet interface with live cell updates
Chat panel showing human and agent messages
Code snippet showing state locking logic
Architecture diagram explaining the collaboration substrate

defects.observed: Spreadsheet cell values and small labels in the chat panel are somewhat difficult to read at standard resolution.
defects.fix: Slightly increase the default zoom level of the UI during recording.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Watch how Noderoom handles concurrent edits between humans and AI agents using a robust collaboration substrate instead of simple chat loops.
